entities.name: Breach of Implied Warranty of Habitability
entities.description: The tenant's defense that the landlord breached an implied warranty to maintain the premises in habitable condition, as measured by housing code standards, thereby suspending the tenant's obligation to pay rent.

relief_sought: Defeat landlord's action for possession for nonpayment of rent
Recoupment or set-off of rent owed

entities.name: Offer of Proof Regarding Housing Code Violations
entities.description: The tenants' proffered evidence of approximately 1500 violations of the Housing Regulations at Clifton Terrace, some directly and some indirectly affecting their premises, to establish a course of conduct violating the code.
entities.source_quote: They offered to prove "[t]hat there are approximately 1500 violations of the Housing Regulations of the District of Columbia in the building at Clifton Terrace, where Defendant resides some affecting the premises of this Defendant directly, others indirectly, and all tending to establish a course of conduct of violation of the Housing Regulations to the damage of Defendants * * *."

entities.name: Landlord's Action for Possession for Nonpayment of Rent
entities.description: A summary proceeding initiated by the landlord in the Landlord and Tenant Branch of the Court of General Sessions to evict tenants for failing to pay rent.

steps: Court determines if rent obligation was suspended by landlord's breach

entities.name: Recognition of Implied Warranty and Remand for Trial
entities.description: The court reversed the lower courts and held that a warranty of habitability, measured by housing code standards, is implied by law into urban dwelling leases. The cases were remanded for the tenants to have an opportunity to prove their allegations of housing code violations as a defense to nonpayment of rent.

entities.name: Housing Regulations of the District of Columbia
entities.description: A comprehensive regulatory scheme establishing standards for housing, assigning maintenance duties between landlords and tenants, and providing for inspections and penalties. The court holds these regulations imply a warranty of habitability into covered leases.

type_specific_fields.citation: Housing Regulations of the District of Columbia (1956)

entities.name: Court of General Sessions Rule 4(c)
entities.description: A procedural rule allowing tenants in nonpayment of rent suits to "set up an equitable defense or claim by way of recoupment or set-off in an amount equal to the rent claim."

type_specific_fields.citation: Rule 4(c) of the Landlord and Tenant Branch of the Court of General Sessions

section_b.chunk_text_preview: Javins v. First National Realty Corp., 428 F.2d 1071 (D.C. Cir. 1970) :: Justia Javins v. First National Realty Corp., 428 F.2d 1071 (D.C. Cir. 1970) Annotate this Case U.S. Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit - 428 F.2d 1071 (D.C. Cir. 1970) Argued January 16, 1970Decided May 7, 1
